items.title: A six-month slump for a former multibagger: What is going on with Eternal?
items.description: Eternal, once a star stock, faces a slump. Competition in food delivery and quick commerce intensifies. Valuations were high, and a leadership change added uncertainty. While core businesses improve, investors seek predictable earnings. The company's strong cash position offers long-term support. Analysts suggest cautious accumulation.

items.title: IRFC shares in focus as government plans to divest 4% via OFS. Check floor price, other details
items.description: The government will sell up to 4% of its stake in Indian Railway Finance Corporation. The Offer for Sale begins Wednesday for institutional investors and Thursday for retail investors. The floor price is set at Rs 104 per share. IRFC is the funding arm for Indian Railways. The company recently reported its highest-ever quarterly profit.

items.title: Waaree Energies among 4 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
items.description: On February 24, four stocks from the Nifty500 universe that gained over 2% were highlighted in the RSI Trending Up scan, based on data from StockEdge.com. It is an uptrend signal if the RSI value has crossed above 50 from below. “RSI trending up” means that the Relative Strength Index (RSI) values are increasing, suggesting strengthening momentum in the price of the stock. This can indicate potential upward movement and may be used by traders to identify buying opportunities.

items.title: IT stock crash wipes out Rs 1.2 lakh crore for LIC & mutual funds in bloodbath not seen since 2008
items.description: India's top financial institutions, LIC and mutual funds, have suffered significant losses as the Nifty IT index plunged 21%. Fears of AI disrupting the traditional IT labour-arbitrage model are driving this downturn, with analysts questioning the long-term growth prospects of major IT firms. Investors face a dilemma between beaten-down valuations and uncertain future fundamentals.

items.title: Afcons Infrastructure shares in focus after client terminates contract worth €113 million
items.description: Afcons Infrastructure's shares are under scrutiny following a contract termination notice from Société Autoroutière du Gabon. The €113.03 million project for National Road NR1 in Gabon is nearing completion. Afcons disputes the termination, asserting it is inconsistent with contract terms. The company is pursuing legal remedies.

items.title: Infosys, TCS and other IT stocks in focus as multiple Anthropic partnerships ease disruption fears
items.description: Indian IT stocks drew attention after Anthropic announced enterprise partnerships, easing fears of AI-led disruption. Heavy recent selling pushed Nifty IT into oversold territory, prompting contrarian interest. While technical indicators hint at potential stabilisation, analysts warn that downside risks persist, with defined support and resistance levels guiding near-term expectations for investors.

items.title: Waaree, Premier Energies, others in focus as US sets initial solar import duty at 126%
items.description: Indian solar stocks such as Waaree Energies and Premier Energies are set to remain in focus after the US imposed preliminary duties of 126% on solar imports from India. The move, aimed at countering alleged subsidies, could restrict access to the US market and impact export-linked order books.

items.title: Natco Pharma among 7 stocks flash bullish signals, indicating possible uptrend
items.description: On February 24, in the Nifty500 pack, seven stocks came under the bullish scanner of “White Marubozu”, according to StockEdge.com's technical scan data. A White Marubozu is a bullish candlestick pattern in technical analysis. It occurs when the opening price is the lowest for a given period and the closing price is the highest.Represented by a long white (or green) candlestick with no upper shadow and a small or non-existent lower shadow, it indicates strong buying pressure throughout the session. Traders interpret it as a signal that buyers were in control from the opening to the closing, often using such patterns to make informed decisions about future price movements in financial markets.

items.title: Stocks to buy in 2026 for long term: IOC, Bharti Airtel among 5 stocks that could give 10-40% return
items.description: Brokerages have turned constructive on select largecaps and midcaps across oil & gas, telecom, capital goods, NBFC and IT services, highlighting meaningful upside potential from current levels.We have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources:

items.title: Yashhtej Industries IPO set for BSE SME debut. Here's what GMP indicates ahead of listing
items.description: Yashhtej Industries is set to list on the BSE SME platform on February 25 with a flat debut expected, as GMP stands at 0%. The company, involved in soybean crude oil and de-oiled cake production, saw its IPO subscribed 1.37 times. Recent financial performance shows significant profit growth, with funds earmarked for capital expenditure and working capital.

items.title: Negative Breakout: These 12 stocks cross below their 200 DMAs
items.description: In the Nifty500 pack, 12 stocks' close prices crossed below their 200 DMA (Daily Moving Averages) on February 24, according to stockedge.com's technical scan data. Trading below the 200 DMA is considered a negative signal because it indicates that the stock's price is below its long-term trend line. The 200 DMA is used as a key indicator by traders for determining the overall trend in a particular stock. Take a look:

items.title: Learn to live with volatility – and still invest: 6 mid-cap stocks from different segments of a sector, with upside potential of up to 47%
items.description: What is narrative and what is reality? A research report has dropped out of the blue, contending that consumption patterns across the world are going to change – because of AI. It says there will be a downtrade in consumption in all possible ways. Lower demand for everything. Now, if everything is going to be hit by AI, will AI stocks or companies be able to grow? This is not the first time that we are reading such narratives about the changes that will be wrought by AI. But there are some sectors where demand cannot diminish.

items.title: Markets tumble with IT shares hit by renewed anxiety over AI impact
items.description: AI company Anthropic's blog post triggered a significant sell-off in Indian IT stocks, causing benchmark indices to fall sharply. The Nifty IT index plunged 4.7%, reflecting investor anxiety over AI's potential to automate legacy system maintenance, as seen with IBM's substantial decline. Analysts anticipate further downside for the IT sector.

items.title: GIFT Nifty up 70 points; here's the trading setup for today's session
items.description: Indian benchmark indices experienced a sharp decline, ending their two-session rally due to the monthly Nifty F&O expiry. Analysts observe Nifty consolidating within a specific range, with a breakout or breakdown expected to signal the next directional trend. Volatility is anticipated to persist amidst global uncertainties.

items.title: Stocks in news: IRFC, Voltas, IDFC First Bank, Alembic Pharma, Lupin
items.description: Markets declined sharply on Tuesday due to weak global cues, with the Nifty retesting crucial support near 25,400. Several stocks like IRFC, Voltas, IDFC First Bank, Alembic Pharma, and Lupin are in focus today due to significant news developments, including government stake sales, price hike expectations, and USFDA approvals.

items.title: Govt to divest up to 4% in IRFC via OFS at Rs 104/ Share
items.description: The government is divesting up to a 4% stake in Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) through an offer for sale. The divestment begins with a 2% stake sale, with an additional 2% green shoe option available. The offer opens for non-retail investors on Wednesday and retail investors on Thursday, with a floor price of ₹104 per share.

items.title: Rs 3 lakh crore added! 5 key factors behind 700-point Sensex surge, Nifty above 25,600
items.description: Indian stock markets saw a significant rebound on Wednesday. The Sensex and Nifty 50 indices surged, recovering from a previous day's sharp decline. This rally boosted the market capitalization of BSE-listed firms substantially. Key sectors like IT and Metal led the gains. Several major companies contributed to the upward movement, indicating a positive market sentiment.

items.title: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warns AI and 'dumb things' can trigger a 2008-like crisis
items.description: Jamie Dimon warned markets resemble pre-2008 conditions, citing risky behaviour, elevated asset prices and AI-driven disruption. He said people are doing “dumb things” to make money, warned of cyclical downturns ahead, and cautioned that enthusiasm around artificial intelligence could destabilise software and technology sectors globally over time.

items.title: Stocks to buy: Adani Ports, HAL, Titan among Prabhudas Lilladher's 14 top picks
items.description: Prabhudas Lilladher Capital in its brokerage note highlighted nine largecaps and five small and midcap stocks as its top picks. The domestic brokerage reduced its 12-month target for Nifty 50 by nearly 3% to 27,958 (implying 10% rise from current level) amid global geopolitical tensions and uncertainty around tariff related issues. It remains overweight on banks, diversified financials, healthcare, consumer, auto and capital goods/defense.

items.title: Vedanta shares rise 5% as BofA upgrades stock to Buy, raises target price by 75%. Here’s why
items.description: Vedanta Ltd shares surged upto 5% on Wednesday after BofA Securities upgraded the stock to “Buy” from “Neutral” and raised its target price sharply to Rs 840 from Rs 480. The brokerage turned positive on the back of a stronger outlook for aluminium and supportive silver prices, along with an estimated FY27 dividend yield of over 6%.

items.title: IBM stock in spotlight after sharp moves. Is volatility creating opportunity?
items.description: IBM shares saw a sharp 13% drop as fears of AI disrupting legacy systems resurfaced, wiping out significant market value. While some see this as an overreaction, the market debates AI's impact on IBM's core businesses. Analysts suggest resilient profit pools and IBM's AI integration strategy could offer a tactical entry point for investors.

items.title: Sensex rallies over 700 points, Nifty above 25,600 as IT stocks bounce back
items.description: Indian stock markets opened higher on Wednesday. Tech stocks bounced back after yesterday's sharp decline. Sensex and Nifty 50 saw gains. IT and Metal sectors led the recovery. Foreign investors were net sellers, while domestic investors bought equities. US markets also closed higher. Crude oil prices increased amid supply concerns.

items.title: Once a 150% multibagger, One Mobikwik shares now trade 26% below IPO price. Time to exit?
items.description: Fintech firm One MobiKwik Systems saw its stock surge on BSE's approval for its subsidiary's broking operations, though gains were mostly trimmed. The company, which offers digital wallets and payment services, has faced prolonged underperformance since its IPO. MobiKwik returned to profitability in the last quarter, reporting its highest revenue in six quarters.

items.title: Buy, Sell or Hold: Morgan Stanley remains Underweight on SRF; Elara Capital upgrades GRSE to Reduce from Sell
items.description: Brokerage activity remained stock specific with Morgan Stanley retaining an Underweight rating on SRF, Elara Capital upgrading GRSE to Reduce from Sell, and Motilal Oswal reiterating a Buy on Polycab India. Analysts cited segment specific earnings trends, order visibility and margin recovery as key drivers behind their revised outlooks.

items.title: Dr. Reddy's Labs shares in focus after US SEC closes probe into bribery claims against firm
items.description: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories shares are in focus today. The US SEC has concluded its investigation into allegations of improper payments to healthcare professionals. The regulator has decided not to recommend any enforcement action against the company. This development follows an internal probe launched by Dr. Reddy's in 2020. The company fully cooperated with the authorities during the investigation.

items.title: Angel One 1:10 stock split tomorrow: What shareholders should know
items.description: Angel One has set February 26 as the record date for its 1:10 stock split, a move aimed at enhancing liquidity and accessibility for retail investors. Shareholders holding the stock on this date will receive 10 shares for every one held, with the face value of each share reduced.

items.title: Anthropic's 'misanthropic' moment for Indian IT! Claude parent's valuation tops the whole industry
items.description: Amid the rapid rise of artificial intelligence, AI firm Anthropic — valued at about $380 billion — has overtaken the combined market capitalisation of India’s top IT companies, including TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL Technologies and Tech Mahindra, which together are worth around $240 billion.

items.title: Waaree Energies says US manufacturing to insulate 126% solar import duty blow. Check more details
items.description: Waaree Energies Share: The United States has imposed significant preliminary duties on solar imports from India, Indonesia, and Laos. This action follows a determination that these countries unfairly subsidize their solar manufacturing sectors. Waaree Energies, an Indian company, expects its expanding US manufacturing to mitigate the impact of these tariffs.

items.title: Nifty near 28,000 by Feb 2027? Prabhudas Lilladher says current consolidation setting stage for next rally
items.description: Domestic brokerage Prabhudas Lilladher expects the Nifty 50 to hit nearly 28,000 in 12 months, saying India’s consolidation phase is paving the way for the next rally. It cites strong policy momentum, major trade deals, resilient corporate earnings and a multi-year compounding cycle supporting medium-term market growth.

items.title: Cement stocks: Will they be sustainably re-rated this time? 16 stocks catering to different regions with wide-ranging upside potential
items.description: When a sector undergoes restructuring and consolidation, the chances are that a set of winners will emerge. Cement is a sector which has probably seen most mergers and acquisitions in the last few years. So, who will the winners be? That’s difficult to predict, but it is time to have these companies on your watchlist. Check out Stock Reports Plus, powered by Refinitiv, for price targets of over 4,000 listed stocks along with detailed company analysis focusing on five key components - earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk and price momentum to generate standardised scores. SR+ Reports is a complimentary offering to ETPrime members.

items.title: More trouble for broader markets? CLSA sees high EPS downgrade risks in smallcaps, here's why
items.description: International brokerage CLSA has said that it continues to favour large caps, but sees more EPS downgrades for small cap stocks.

items.title: TCS can offer Rs 35 dividend, share buyback soon, says CLSA after Rs 3,593 target price
items.description: CLSA has maintained an Outperform rating on Tata Consultancy Services with a target price of Rs 3,593, implying 39% upside. The brokerage sees scope for a Rs 35 Q4 dividend and a potential buyback. However, AI disruption concerns and macroeconomic risks continue to weigh on IT sector valuations.

items.title: Multibaggers: FIIs raise stakes in 11 high-growth stocks; rally up to 268% in a year
items.description: It’s always exciting to track high-growth stocks. They grow fast, with rising sales and profits, but the real boost comes when big institutional investors, especially FIIs, increase their stake in them. It adds an extra spark. FII decisions are usually backed by deep research, detailed analysis, and long-term conviction. Broad-Based GrowthIn the BSE1000 universe, around 103 companies (excluding banking and financial stocks) delivered more than 10% year-on-year growth in both sales and profits in the December '25 quarter. That alone is impressive. But there’s more. FIIs raised their stake in these companies during the December quarter compared to their September holdings.Out of this filtered list, about 34 stocks have already rallied between 50% and a staggering 265% over the past year. Among them are 11 true multibaggers that have significantly multiplied investor wealth. These high-growth, FII-favoured stocks may be worth keeping on your radar. (Data Source: ACE Equity)

items.title: Gainers & Losers: Concord Biotech, IRFC among 6 top movers on Wednesday
​Union Bank Of India among 6 Financial Services stocks that hit 52-week high; rally up to 15% in a month​
items.description: On Wednesday, the benchmark Sensex rose by 50 points to close at 82,276. Amid this uptrend, six stocks in the BSE Financial Services index hit their 52-week highs, reaching their highest levels in the past year. Such milestones are often viewed by investors as bullish indicators, reflecting strong upward momentum and the possibility of further gains.

items.title: Market Wrap: Sensex ends 50 pts higher, Nifty above 25,400 as bank stocks keep rally in check
items.description: At close, Sensex was up a little over 50 points at 82,276. This marks a fall of around 682 points from its intraday high of 82,958, which it had hit in the morning amid strong buying in IT stocks. Nifty 50 ended the session around 58 points higher at 25,482, after falling over 170 points from its day’s high of 25,653.

items.title: Zerodha's Nithin Kamath bats for loan against shares facility to service high interest loans
items.description: Nithin Kamath said Zerodha’s Loan Against Shares business has crossed Rs 500 crore, calling it a secured way to repay high-interest debt. Offered via Zerodha Capital, LAS provides lower rates. He also clarified DP charges and urged investors to check hidden costs.

items.title: Tech, AI to play big role in strengthening mkt surveillance, improving risk management: Sebi chief
items.description: Sebi chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey on Wednesday said that technology, including artificial intelligence, will play an expanding role in strengthening surveillance, enhancing transparency and deepening investor awareness.

items.title: Indian stocks set for another lacklustre year amid geopolitical uncertainty: Poll
items.description: Optimism about Indian stocks among equity strategists has waned from three months ago, although analysts ​still expect benchmark indices to scale new highs ​by mid-year, according to a Reuters poll.

items.title: Two Trades for Today: A major 2-wheeler maker for a 4.3% upmove, a large-cap pharmaceutical stock for about 6% rise
items.description: Our technical analysts identify select stocks that may gain momentum even in volatile markets. Here are the technical calls for today.

items.title: Govt to skip green shoe option as IRFC OFS not fully subscribed
items.description: Government will not exercise the oversubscription option for the Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) offer for sale (OFS) after the issue failed to get fully subscribed on the opening day.

items.title: HSBC India profit up 11% led by corporate, institutional business
items.description: HSBC reported an 11% rise in India profit before tax to $1.86 billion in 2025, led by strong corporate and institutional banking growth. India became its third most profitable market as China earnings slumped, while global profits declined despite beating expectations.
